title: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtim
stakeholder_needs: Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner for home use. Runtime >= 20 min, weight < 1.5 kg, EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders include: end users (want lightweight, easy handling, sufficient suction/runtime, low noise, simple emptying and maintenance), purchasers/retailers (competitive price, strong specs, clear labeling), manufacturer/engineering (battery selection, thermal management, motor efficiency, durability), compliance/legal (EU safety and regulatory conformity, documentation), service/support (replaceable filters/battery, fault diagnostics), and charging/base ecosystem users (safe, convenient docking/charging, cord management). Key requirements already stated: runtime ≥20 min, mass <1.5 kg, indoor operating 10–40°C, EU market compliance, plug-in charging base. Likely derived/related requirements to engineer: suction performance targets (e.g., airflow/pressure, pickup on common debris), battery capacity/chemistry and charge time, noise limits, dustbin volume and filtration (HEPA?), ergonomics (center of mass, grip), safety (electrical, mechanical, battery, charger), reliability (cycle life, drop resistance), and maintainability (washable filters, consumables availability). Regulatory scope may include CE marking under relevant directives/regs (e.g., LVD/EMC/RED if wireless, RoHS/REACH, WEEE, battery regulation/transport), plus harmonized standards for household vacuum cleaners and charger safety.
clarification_questions: What cleaning performance is required (target surfaces like hard floors vs carpets, typical debris/pet hair), and is there a minimum suction/airflow or pickup metric to meet?
What battery/charging expectations should be met (max charge time, desired battery type—Li-ion only?, removable vs built-in battery, and required cycle life/warranty target)?
Are there additional constraints such as maximum noise level, dustbin capacity, filtration level (e.g., HEPA), and any wireless features (Bluetooth/Wi‑Fi) that would change regulatory scope (RED)?

table.title: DFMEA – Battery-powered handheld vacuum cleaner (EU, runtime ≥20 min, mass <1.5 kg)
rows: Battery pack (Li-ion cells + BMS)
Provide energy for ≥20 min runtime; safe operation 10–40°C
Thermal runaway / venting during use or charge
Fire/smoke; injury; property damage; recall; non-compliance
Safety Critical
Internal short, overcharge, overcurrent, cell damage, insufficient thermal design, BMS fault
Cell qualification; BMS OVP/OCP/OTP; thermal simulation; pack fusing; supplier audits
Pack abuse tests; charge/discharge thermal testing; end-of-line BMS functional test
Add redundant temperature sensing; improve heat spreading/venting; tighten cell screening criteria
Battery Systems Engineer
Post-change validation: abuse tests passed; no venting events; BMS fault coverage improved
Battery pack (Li-ion cells)
Deliver ≥20 min runtime at rated suction
Insufficient capacity / high internal resistance leading to short runtime
Customer dissatisfaction; returns; fails runtime requirement
Performance
Cell lot variability, aging, poor cell matching, high load current, cold temp sensitivity
Cell grading/binning; capacity margin; motor power optimization; pack design review
Runtime verification test; impedance check; periodic life testing
Increase capacity margin or improve motor efficiency; implement tighter incoming cell acceptance limits
Battery Systems Engineer
Runtime ≥20 min met across production lots; reduced returns for low runtime
Charger / charging base
Charge battery safely; compliant to EU LVD/EMC; user-friendly docking
Overcharge or wrong charge profile
Battery damage, overheating, safety incident, reduced cycle life
Safety Critical
Charger control IC failure, wrong firmware, incorrect adapter, poor CC/CV regulation
Use certified power supply; charge algorithm review; derating; protective circuitry
Charge profile measurement; EOL charger functional test; BMS charge cutoff verification
Add secondary hardware cutoff; implement charger/BMS handshake validation; expand EMC/LVD pre-compliance testing
Power Electronics Engineer
Charge voltage/current stays within limits; no overheating in worst-case testing
Charging contacts / dock interface
Provide reliable electrical connection during charge
Intermittent contact / high contact resistance
No charge or slow charge; heating at contacts; user complaints
Reliability
Misalignment, spring fatigue, corrosion, contamination, low contact force
Contact material plating spec; mechanical alignment features; ingress shielding; design FMEA review
Contact resistance test; docking cycle test; user-indicator charge confirmation
Increase contact wipe action and force; add contamination shields; implement docking misalignment detection in UI
Mechanical Engineer
Docking cycles pass; reduced field complaints of no-charge; contact temperature within spec
Motor (BLDC)
Provide airflow/suction with acceptable noise and efficiency
Motor bearing wear leading to increased friction/noise or stall
Reduced suction; high noise; overheating; early failure
Reliability
Bearing contamination, insufficient lubrication, misalignment, poor balancing, ingress
Bearing selection; contamination control; rotor balancing; airflow path design to reduce dust ingress
Endurance test; noise/vibration test; current draw monitoring
Add upstream debris screen; improve bearing seals; adjust balancing tolerance; add stall protection tuning
Motor Design Engineer
Endurance hours increased; noise within target; fewer stalls in dust-ingress tests
Motor control electronics
Control motor speed/power; protect against stall/overcurrent
Controller overcurrent protection fails
Overheating; PCB damage; battery stress; potential smoke; non-compliance
Safety Critical
Firmware bug, MOSFET short, sense resistor drift, poor thermal design
Hardware current limiting; thermal design review; code review and MISRA compliance
EOL functional test; overcurrent fault injection; thermal imaging under load
Add redundant current sensing; improve MOSFET derating/heat sinking; expand fault-injection coverage
Embedded Systems Engineer
Fault injection shows safe shutdown; controller temps reduced; no component overstress
Suction path / impeller
Generate suction and airflow; maintain pickup performance
Impeller clogging by debris/hair
Loss of suction; user frustration; potential motor overload
Performance
Narrow flow path, insufficient pre-separation, static adhesion, rough surfaces
Design for clean-out access; anti-clog geometry; hair management features
Clogging test with hair/debris; airflow/pressure monitoring; motor current monitoring
Widen critical passages; add hair-removal comb and smoother surfaces; improve user-accessible cleanout
Airflow Engineer
Clog frequency reduced in standardized debris tests; suction maintained over bin fill levels
Filter (HEPA/primary)
Capture dust/allergens; protect motor; maintain airflow
Filter media tears or gasket bypass
Dust leakage; allergen exposure; motor contamination; regulatory risk for claims
Safety/Regulatory
Material defect, poor pleat bonding, improper seating, gasket deformation
Supplier PPAP; media strength spec; poka-yoke seating features; gasket material validation
Leak test; visual inspection; differential pressure test
Add 100% leak check at assembly; strengthen pleat bonds; improve filter frame rigidity
Quality Engineer
Leak rate reduced; assembly errors decreased; filtration claims supported by test data
Filter (washable)
Maintain airflow over life; easy maintenance
Filter clogs rapidly / not washable as claimed
Reduced suction; increased noise; customer dissatisfaction; higher support burden
Performance
Undersized filter area, user mis-maintenance, moisture retention, fine dust loading
Increase filter area; clear instructions; select media with better dust-holding capacity
Airflow life test; user validation; differential pressure trending
Increase effective filter area; add maintenance indicator based on pressure/current; revise IFU with clear drying time
Product Engineer
Longer time-to-clog in tests; improved user maintenance compliance; fewer suction-related returns
Dustbin / emptying mechanism
Contain debris; enable hygienic emptying; prevent leaks
Dustbin latch fails to secure / opens unintentionally
Spillage; mess; user dissatisfaction; potential injury if dropped
Safety/Customer
Weak latch geometry, material creep, tolerance stack, fatigue, improper assembly
Latch design margin; material selection; assembly poka-yoke; tolerance analysis
Drop test; latch cycle test; pull-force verification
Reinforce latch; add secondary catch; tighten tolerances; implement 100% latch engagement check
Mechanical Engineer
Latch cycles pass; no unintended openings in drop tests; reduced spillage complaints
Housing / structure
Protect internals; maintain mass <1.5 kg; withstand drops
Housing crack/break after drop
Sharp edges; internal damage; loss of function; safety hazard
Safety
Brittle plastic, poor ribbing, weld line weakness, fastener boss failure
Material impact testing; structural simulation; rib/boss design guidelines
Drop testing (multiple orientations); visual inspection; torque audits
Increase impact modifier or change resin; add ribs/fillets; optimize fastener boss design
Mechanical Engineer
Drop performance improved; no sharp fractures; mass remains <1.5 kg
Seal (gaskets/O-rings between bin, filter, motor)
Maintain airtight system for suction and dust containment
Seal deformation/leak causing air bypass
Reduced suction; dust leakage; noise increase; motor contamination
Performance
Poor material set, compression loss, assembly misalignment, chemical exposure, wear
Seal material validation; compression set testing; assembly guides; poka-yoke features
Vacuum leak test; airflow performance test; visual inspection
Switch to lower compression-set material; add alignment features; implement 100% vacuum leak test at EOL
Manufacturing Engineer
Leak rate reduced; suction variability decreased; improved consistency across builds
UI (buttons/indicators)
Allow user to turn on/off, select modes, see charge/fault status
Button failure or stuck input
Cannot operate or unexpected operation; user frustration; potential safety if cannot shut off
Safety/Customer
Poor switch rating, contamination ingress, PCB flex, poor debounce firmware
IP sealing around switch; component derating; mechanical support; firmware review
Button functional test; HALT; field diagnostic logging
Upgrade switch spec; add ingress barrier; implement long-press hard-off and watchdog; improve debounce
UI/UX Engineer
Reduced button failures in life test; hard-off works under fault; improved usability metrics
UI / labeling & documentation (EU compliance)
Provide compliant markings, warnings, and instructions (CE, WEEE, battery)
Incorrect/missing regulatory labeling or safety instructions
Non-compliance; customs hold; fines; recalls; brand damage
Regulatory
Artwork/version control errors, supplier print changes, translation issues
Document control; regulatory checklist; label proof approval; supplier change notification
Incoming label inspection; compliance audit; technical file review
Implement barcode/version verification at line; add final compliance gate prior to release; strengthen translation review
Regulatory Compliance Engineer
Audit findings closed; correct labels verified on all SKUs; no customs/market surveillance issues
Electrical wiring / connectors internal
Deliver power/signals reliably under vibration and handling
Connector loosening / wire chafing leading to open/short
Intermittent operation; overheating; potential smoke; service cost
Safety
Insufficient strain relief, sharp edges, vibration, poor routing, assembly error
Harness routing rules; strain relief; edge protection; connector latching features
Continuity test; vibration test; visual inspection; thermal monitoring
Add strain relief clamp; improve routing clearances; use higher-retention connectors; add assembly training and aids
Electrical Engineer
No intermittent faults in vibration test; reduced hot spots; improved assembly yield
